Summarize cell-level metadata to donor / patient level

SummarizeMetadata.Rd

Groups cells by one or more ID columns (e.g. donor ID, sample ID) and returns a wide data frame with one row per unique ID combination.

Usage

SummarizeMetadata(
  data,
  id_columns,
  numeric_func = mean,
  numeric_na_rm = TRUE,
  pivot = FALSE
)

Arguments

data

A Seurat object or a plain data frame / tibble.

id_columns

Character vector of one or more column names that uniquely identify a donor or sample (e.g. c("Donor.ID") or c("Donor.ID", "Visit")). One output row is produced per unique combination.

numeric_func

Function to summarize numeric columns. Must accept a numeric vector and return a single value. Default mean. Common alternatives: median, max, function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE).

numeric_na_rm

Logical. Pass na.rm = TRUE to numeric_func. Default TRUE.

pivot

Logical. If TRUE, the result is pivoted to long format (one row per ID × variable combination). Default FALSE (wide).

Value

A data frame (wide by default). Always includes n_cells (number of cells per ID combination).

Details

Column handling:

Numeric columns

Summarized with numeric_func (default: mean).

Categorical columns - simple

When every cell in an ID group shares the same value (e.g. every cell from donor D1 has Sex = "Male"), that single value is returned as-is.

Categorical columns - complex

When cells in an ID group have multiple values (e.g. a donor has several cell types), the result is a formatted string listing all values and their cell counts in descending order: "CD4 T (n=1 420), NK (n=380), B (n=200)".

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# One ID column
donor_df <- SummarizeMetadata(SeuratObj, id_columns = "Donor.ID")

# Multiple ID columns (one row per donor × visit combination)
donor_df <- SummarizeMetadata(SeuratObj,
  id_columns   = c("Donor.ID", "Visit"),
  numeric_func = median)

# Long format for easy downstream plotting
long_df <- SummarizeMetadata(SeuratObj, id_columns = "Donor.ID", pivot = TRUE)
} # }
